# Story: Bugfix 10.4 — PosterCard Hover State Align with `.pen` `Component/PosterCardHover` (MQbvp)

Status: ready-for-dev

<!-- Note: Validation is optional. Run validate-create-story for quality check before dev-story. -->
<!-- Generated by SM Bob /create-story (YOLO) on 2026-05-08. -->
<!-- Implements: Component/PosterCardHover (MQbvp) per Rule 21 (project-context.md L654). -->
<!-- Origin: Party Mode 2026-05-08 root-cause discovery. Sally (UX), Bob (SM),
     Winston (Architect), Amelia (DEV), Murat (TEA) consensus: HoverPreviewCard.tsx
     was independently invented and diverges from .pen Component/PosterCardHover.
     Real bug = perceptual-proximity failure, not the original "viewport-flip"
     framing. Story name preserved (sprint-status key) but scope pivoted. -->

## Story

As a desktop user browsing a poster grid (homepage, library, search results, TMDb explore blocks),
I want hover feedback to appear **inside the card itself** — at the cursor's natural focal point — instead of as a separate panel that drops below the title/year row,
so that hovering feels immediate and discoverable, the action affordances (▶ play, ⋯ menu, select) are exactly where my eyes already are, and the experience matches the project's `.pen` design contract for `Component/PosterCardHover` (node `MQbvp`) instead of the unsanctioned floating-overview panel that crept in.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. **[@contract-v1] In-card hover overlay matches `Component/PosterCardHover` (MQbvp).** Given a `PosterCard` rendered on `lg:` breakpoint or wider, when the user hovers (`mouseenter` on the `<Link>` element), then the card's image area MUST display an in-card overlay that:
   - **Center:** a large circular play affordance (`▶`) with semi-transparent backdrop, visible only on hover, indicating the click-to-detail action.
   - **Top-right:** the existing kebab menu button (`⋯`, `data-testid="poster-menu-button"`) — relocated from its current top-left position to top-right per MQbvp. Still visible only on hover via `lg:group-hover:opacity-100`. Still gated by `onMenuClick` prop.
   - **Top-left:** existing `data-testid="selection-checkbox"` (when `selectable={true}`) stays at top-left per MQbvp. No new affordance added when `selectable={false}` — the corner is empty.
   - **Bottom-left:** title + year (when present) overlaid on the image with a dark gradient backdrop for legibility (white text). The existing below-image title/year row (`<div className="mt-2">` at PosterCard.tsx:204-210) MAY stay as-is for default-state continuity, but the hover-state in-card overlay is the primary visual.
   - **Bottom-right:** the rating badge (`⭐ 8.5`-style) — relocated from its current `bottom-2 left-2` position to `bottom-2 right-2` per MQbvp. Visible whenever `voteAverage > 0` (not gated by hover — same visibility as today).

   Verification: DEV MUST capture a screenshot of `Component/PosterCardHover` via Pencil MCP `get_screenshot(nodeId="MQbvp")` and side-by-side compare against the rendered hover state in `pnpm nx serve web` BEFORE marking the task complete. Save the comparison artifact to `_bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/bugfix-10-4-hover-comparison.md`.

2. **`HoverPreviewCard.tsx` and its spec are deleted from the repo.** Given the floating-below-card overview/genre/original-title preview was the symptom of the perceptual-proximity bug, when this story closes, then both `apps/web/src/components/media/HoverPreviewCard.tsx` AND `apps/web/src/components/media/HoverPreviewCard.spec.tsx` MUST be removed (`git rm`), AND `PosterCard.tsx` MUST no longer import the symbol (line 7 import removed, lines 213-220 render block removed). `grep -r "HoverPreviewCard" apps/web/` must return zero matches after the change.

3. **Rule 21 header comment present on PosterCard.tsx.** Given Rule 21 (Component-to-Design Node Traceability) ratified in commit 6c0cbf2 (project-context.md L654), when this story lands, then `apps/web/src/components/media/PosterCard.tsx` MUST include a header comment (top of file, before imports) with the exact form:
   ```
   // Implements: Component/PosterCardHover (MQbvp)
   // Source: ux-design.pen (Pencil app)
   ```
   This is verified by `grep -E "// Implements: Component/PosterCardHover \(MQbvp\)" apps/web/src/components/media/PosterCard.tsx` returning a hit. Manual SM-template enforcement is the Phase 1 gate per Rule 21; automated ESLint enforcement is deferred to story 19-3.

4. **Zero floating elements outside the card boundary.** Given the perceptual-proximity bug was caused by a `position: absolute` element rendered as a sibling of the card's image area but visually placed outside its flow, when the hover state is active, then NO DOM element with `position: absolute` AND `top-full` (or any positioning that places content below the `<Link>` element's bounding box) MUST exist in the rendered tree. Inspectable in DevTools — every element belonging to the hover state MUST sit within the card's image `<div>` (the one with `aspect-[2/3]` class) and be bounded by it.

5. **Click on card preserves navigation behavior.** Given `PosterCard` is a `<Link>` to `/media/$type/$id`, when the user clicks anywhere on the card (image, overlay, or below-image title row) NOT on the kebab menu or selection-checkbox, then the click MUST navigate to the detail page exactly as today. The new center-play overlay is decorative — it does NOT have its own `onClick`; clicking the play button propagates to the parent `<Link>`. AC verified by existing test `'links to correct movie detail page'` and `'links to correct tv detail page'` (PosterCard.spec.tsx:141-152) staying green.

6. **Mobile (`< lg` breakpoint) shows default-state only — no hover overlay.** Given the hover affordances are designed for pointer-precise input only, when the viewport is narrower than the Tailwind `lg:` breakpoint (default 1024px), then the entire in-card hover overlay layer MUST be hidden via `hidden lg:block` (or equivalent `lg:group-hover:` gating). Touch users tap to navigate to the detail page — same as the existing `lg:` gate on the deleted HoverPreviewCard. AC verified by a new test asserting the overlay layer has `hidden lg:block` (or similar) classes via DOM inspection.

7. **Existing PosterCard.spec.tsx hover tests are migrated, not deleted.** Given PosterCard.spec.tsx currently has a `'Hover Interaction'` describe block (lines 178-230) with 4 tests asserting `hover-preview-card`, overview text, and genre tags appear on `mouseEnter`, when this story lands, then those tests MUST be REPLACED (not deleted) with new tests asserting the in-card hover state per AC #1: the center play overlay, repositioned kebab, repositioned rating, and the bottom-left title overlay. New describe block name: `'Hover Interaction (in-card overlay per Component/PosterCardHover MQbvp)'`. Test count delta: -4 old tests, +5 new tests = net +1.

8. **Lint baseline maintained or reduced.** Given Rule 12 (CI Lint) requires `pnpm lint:all` green, when the story lands, then:
   - `pnpm lint:all` MUST stay 0 errors / ≤ 122 warnings (current bugfix-10-2 baseline).
   - ZERO new `as any` casts introduced.
   - ZERO new `as ReturnType<typeof X>` casts introduced (use `Partial<UseQueryResult<...>>` typed-mock pattern from bugfix-10-2 CR M3 if mocks needed — this story is unlikely to need new mocks).
   - `pnpm exec prettier --check` clean on all modified files.

9. **Test gate green.** Given Rule 9 (Test Co-location) + Rule 16 (Test Assertion Quality) + the bugfix story precedent, when the story lands, then:
   - `pnpm nx test web`: PASS (~1770/1770 tests, +1 net per AC #7).
   - `pnpm nx test api`: PASS (no backend touched, but full gate per Epic 9 Retro AI-1 mandate).
   - All new assertions use specific matchers (`toBeInTheDocument`, `toBeAttached` for hover-CSS-dependent elements, `toHaveClass` for class-presence checks). NO `toBeTruthy()` for DOM presence; NO `toBeVisible()` for elements gated by `lg:group-hover:opacity-100`-style classes (use `toBeAttached` per Rule 16).

10. **Out-of-scope (deferred to follow-ups):** the following are NOT addressed by this story:
    - **Visual regression baseline (story 19-4):** Playwright `toHaveScreenshot()` baseline for the new hover state will be set up by story 19-4 once Sally signs off the rendered output. This story's AC #1 verification is a one-time side-by-side comparison artifact, not a CI-enforced pixel snapshot.
    - **ESLint enforcement of Rule 21 header (story 19-3):** This story manually adds the `// Implements:` comment per AC #3. The custom ESLint rule that enforces it across all components ships in story 19-3.
    - **Other PosterCard call-site audits:** The `PosterCard` component is used in homepage `ExploreBlock`, library grid, search results, and TMDb detail page. Each call site MUST continue to work after this story (regression-checked via existing tests + AC #5 + AC #6), but no per-call-site visual audits are part of this story. Drift audit story 19-8 will sweep all call sites.
    - **Adding a "favorite/save" affordance:** MQbvp's top-left checkbox shape *could* be reinterpreted as a new "favorite" feature, but this story preserves the existing `selectable`-mode `selection-checkbox` semantics. Net new affordances are out of scope.
    - **Top-right badge collision strategy beyond hover-replacement:** When `selectable={false}` and `onMenuClick` is provided, the kebab moves to top-right and the existing badge cluster (availability/isNew/type) stays at top-right in the default state. The simplest collision strategy is: in hover state, fade out the badge cluster (make it `lg:group-hover:opacity-0`) so the kebab takes over the corner. DEV MAY choose this OR an alternative if Sally signs off — keep the comparison artifact (AC #1) as the spec.

Default state — badges visible. Hover state — badges fade, kebab fades in. Sally signs off via comparison artifact (AC #1).

## Dev Notes

### Why this story scope is what it is

**Original sprint-status framing (2026-04-20)**: "PosterCard hover preview floats below card, gets clipped/forces scroll. UX decides flip-up via Radix Popover vs click-to-expand vs inline expansion."

**Party Mode pivot (2026-05-08)**: Sally discovered that the `.pen` design file already contained `Component/PosterCardHover` (node `MQbvp`) — a complete in-card overlay design. The floating `HoverPreviewCard.tsx` in production was an unsanctioned addition that nobody had cross-referenced against the design. Real bug: perceptual-proximity failure (hover feedback floats outside user's focal area, looks unresponsive). The original "viewport-flip" framing was symptom-level — the position is bad in ALL viewport positions, not just at edges.

**Therefore this story is NOT**: adding a Radix Popover, adding click-to-expand, or designing a new component. It IS: bringing the production code into compliance with the design contract that was always there. This is also the inaugural story under Rule 21 (commit 6c0cbf2) — the manual-enforcement Phase 1 gate.

### Architecture Compliance

- **Rule 1 (Single Backend):** N/A — pure FE story, no backend touched.
- **Rule 5 (TanStack Query for Server State):** N/A — no server state involved.
- **Rule 7 (Error Codes):** N/A — no new error codes.
- **Rule 9 (Test Co-location):** PosterCard.spec.tsx stays co-located with PosterCard.tsx. HoverPreviewCard.spec.tsx is deleted alongside HoverPreviewCard.tsx.
- **Rule 12 (CI Lint):** AC #8 enforces 0 errors, ≤ 122 warnings (bugfix-10-2 baseline).
- **Rule 15 (Pre-commit Self-verification):** No main.go wiring, no DB migration, no Swagger — all N/A. The "self-verification" gate that DOES apply: AC #6 manual UX comparison (Sally signs off) — this is the equivalent of Rule 15's wiring check for design-fidelity.
- **Rule 16 (Test Assertion Quality):** AC #9 enforces specific matchers. Notable: hover-CSS-dependent elements use `toBeAttached` not `toBeVisible` (per Rule 16's CSS hover/transition note) because RTL cannot fire CSS `:hover` events.
- **Rule 18 (API Boundary Case Transformation):** N/A — no API boundaries crossed.
- **Rule 20 (AC Contract Versioning):** AC #1 is `[@contract-v1]` because it introduces a NEW visual contract for the hover state. Future stories that touch PosterCard hover MUST bump v2 + Change Log. Upstream contract grep:
  ```bash
  grep -nE '\[@contract-v[0-9]+\]' _bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/{5-7,5-8,10-4,10-5}*.md
  ```
  Expected: 0 hits referencing PosterCard hover state. Confirmed implicit-v0 per Rule 20 forward-only retrofit. No `confirmed against [@contract-v0]` ack required.
- **Rule 21 (Component-to-Design Node Traceability) — INAUGURAL ENFORCEMENT:** AC #3 is the first story to add the `// Implements:` header. PosterCard becomes the reference example for story 19-3 (ESLint rule) and story 19-8 (sweep). Get this exactly right.
- **Rule 22 (Epic Retro Design-Drift Audit):** Task 6.6 explicitly stamps this story's comparison artifact into `_bmad-output/audit/` for epic-19 aggregation. This is also the first instance of Rule 22's audit trail.

### Cross-Stack Split Check

- Backend tasks: 0
- Frontend tasks: 5 (Task 2 + Task 3 + Task 4 + Task 5 + Task 6 — Task 1 is a design-verification step, Task 7 is closeout)
- Verdict: **Single story** — backend count is 0, well under the >3 threshold.

| `apps/web/src/components/media/HoverPreviewCard.tsx` | **DELETED** (Task 2.1). |

### Implementation sketch — `PosterCard.tsx` hover overlay structure

```tsx
// Implements: Component/PosterCardHover (MQbvp)
// Source: ux-design.pen (Pencil app)

import { useState } from 'react';   // (keep for image-loading state)
import { Link } from '@tanstack/react-router';
import { MoreHorizontal, Check, Play } from 'lucide-react';   // + Play
import { cn } from '../../lib/utils';
// ...rest unchanged...

export function PosterCard(props) {
  const [imageLoaded, setImageLoaded] = useState(false);
  const [imageError, setImageError] = useState(false);
  // ❌ REMOVED: const [isHovered, setIsHovered] = useState(false);

  return (
    <Link
      to="/media/$type/$id"
      params={{ type, id }}
      data-testid="poster-card"
      onClick={handleCardClick}
      className={cn('group relative block rounded-lg', /* ... */)}
      // ❌ REMOVED: onMouseEnter / onMouseLeave (now CSS-driven via group-hover)
    >
      <div className={cn('relative aspect-[2/3] overflow-hidden rounded-lg ...')}>

        {/* ...skeleton, image, fallback unchanged... */}

        {/* Selection checkbox (top-LEFT, only when selectable) — unchanged from current */}
        {selectable && (<div data-testid="selection-checkbox" className="absolute left-2 top-2 z-10 ...">...</div>)}

        {/* Top-RIGHT badge cluster — fade out on hover so kebab takes over */}
        <div className="absolute right-2 top-2 flex items-center gap-1
                        transition-opacity lg:group-hover:opacity-0">
          {/* ...availability + new + metadataSource + type — unchanged... */}
        </div>

        {/* Kebab — RELOCATED top-LEFT → top-RIGHT, hover-only */}
        {onMenuClick && (
          <button
            onClick={(e) => { e.preventDefault(); e.stopPropagation(); onMenuClick(e); }}
            className="absolute right-2 top-2 z-10 rounded-full bg-black/70 p-1.5 text-white
                       opacity-0 transition-opacity hover:bg-black/90 lg:group-hover:opacity-100"
            aria-label="更多選項"
            data-testid="poster-menu-button"
          >
            <MoreHorizontal className="h-4 w-4" />
          </button>
        )}

        {/* CENTER play overlay — NEW, hover-only, decorative (no onClick) */}
        <div
          data-testid="hover-play-overlay"
          aria-hidden="true"
          className="absolute inset-0 hidden items-center justify-center
                     opacity-0 transition-opacity lg:flex lg:group-hover:opacity-100"
        >
          <div className="rounded-full bg-black/60 p-4 backdrop-blur-sm">
            <Play className="h-8 w-8 fill-white text-white" />
          </div>
        </div>

        {/* BOTTOM-LEFT title/year overlay on image — NEW, hover-only */}
        <div
          data-testid="hover-title-overlay"
          className="absolute inset-x-0 bottom-0 hidden bg-gradient-to-t from-black/80 to-transparent
                     px-3 pb-2 pt-8 opacity-0 transition-opacity lg:block lg:group-hover:opacity-100"
        >
          <h3 className="truncate text-sm font-medium text-white">{title}</h3>
          {year && <p className="text-xs text-white/70">{year}</p>}
        </div>

        {/* Rating — RELOCATED bottom-LEFT → bottom-RIGHT */}
        {voteAverage !== undefined && voteAverage > 0 && (
          <div className="absolute bottom-2 right-2 z-10">
            <span className="flex items-center gap-1 rounded bg-black/70 px-2 py-0.5 text-xs text-[var(--warning)]">
              ⭐ {voteAverage.toFixed(1)}
            </span>
          </div>
        )}
      </div>

      {/* Title and year BELOW image — DEFAULT-STATE affordance, unchanged */}
      <div className="mt-2">
        <h3 className="truncate text-sm font-medium text-white">
          <HighlightText text={title} query={highlightQuery} />
        </h3>
        {year && <p className="text-xs text-[var(--text-secondary)]">{year}</p>}
      </div>

      {/* ❌ REMOVED: {isHovered && <HoverPreviewCard .../>} */}
    </Link>
  );
}
```

### Test pattern — hover-class assertion (Task 4.2 Test 1+2)

```typescript
// PosterCard.spec.tsx — new describe block

describe('Hover Interaction (in-card overlay per Component/PosterCardHover MQbvp)', () => {
  it('[P0] center play overlay is in DOM with hover-only visibility classes', () => {
    render(<PosterCard {...defaultProps} />);
    const overlay = screen.getByTestId('hover-play-overlay');
    // Element MUST exist (cannot fire CSS :hover from RTL — use toBeAttached/class check)
    expect(overlay).toBeInTheDocument();
    // Visibility gating MUST be CSS-driven (group-hover) and lg:-only
    expect(overlay).toHaveClass('hidden', 'lg:flex', 'opacity-0', 'lg:group-hover:opacity-100');
  });

  it('[P0] kebab menu is repositioned to top-right (not top-left)', () => {
    const onMenuClick = vi.fn();
    render(<PosterCard {...defaultProps} onMenuClick={onMenuClick} />);
    const kebab = screen.getByTestId('poster-menu-button');
    expect(kebab).toHaveClass('right-2', 'top-2');
    expect(kebab).not.toHaveClass('left-2');
  });

  it('[P0] rating badge is repositioned to bottom-right (not bottom-left)', () => {
    const { container } = render(<PosterCard {...defaultProps} voteAverage={8.5} />);
    const ratingWrapper = container.querySelector('[class*="bottom-2"][class*="right-2"]');
    expect(ratingWrapper).toBeInTheDocument();
    const ratingWrapperLeft = container.querySelector('[class*="bottom-2"][class*="left-2"]');
    expect(ratingWrapperLeft).not.toBeInTheDocument();
  });

  it('[P0] in-card title overlay exists with hover-only visibility classes', () => {
    render(<PosterCard {...defaultProps} />);
    const titleOverlay = screen.getByTestId('hover-title-overlay');
    expect(titleOverlay).toBeInTheDocument();
    expect(titleOverlay).toHaveClass('hidden', 'lg:block', 'opacity-0', 'lg:group-hover:opacity-100');
  });

  it('[P1] HoverPreviewCard is no longer in the DOM at any point', () => {
    render(<PosterCard {...defaultProps} overview="some overview" genreIds={[16]} />);
    expect(screen.queryByTestId('hover-preview-card')).not.toBeInTheDocument();
  });
});
```

### Project Structure Notes

- All edits stay within `apps/web/src/components/media/`. No new directories. No new dependencies (`Play` icon already available from `lucide-react` per existing imports of `MoreHorizontal, Check`).
- Comparison artifacts (`bugfix-10-4-mqbvp-design.png`, `bugfix-10-4-hover-comparison.md`) live alongside other bugfix artifacts under `_bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/`. The `_bmad-output/audit/` symlink (Task 6.6) is the FIRST entry under that path — DEV creates the directory.
- No CSS file edits (Tailwind utility-classes only). No `tailwind.config.js` edits.

### Previous-Story Intelligence

- **bugfix-10-1 (TMDb-ID 404)** — established `Partial<ReturnType<typeof X>>` typed-mock pattern (CR M3) and the strict `as ReturnType<...>` discipline over `as any`. Relevant if any new mocks are needed (unlikely for this story per AC #8, but if tests need a mocked router it MUST follow this pattern).
- **bugfix-10-2 (qBT HTTP status)** — established `Partial<UseQueryResult<...>>` over `as any` for hook mocks. Same precedent. Also: this story's lint baseline reference (122 warnings).
- **bugfix-10-3 (skeleton flicker, Bucket A)** — established the "deletion is the fix" precedent. This story is conceptually similar: we're deleting a component (HoverPreviewCard) rather than fixing it, because the component should never have existed. Both close out cleanly without leaving dead code behind. Bucket A also established the pattern: when a thing shouldn't exist, deleting it IS the regression-prevention.
- **Story 5-7 (selectable mode)** — added `selection-checkbox` top-left + `selectable` prop. We preserve those semantics intact. Selection mode is mutually exclusive with hover affordances (when selecting, you're not browsing; when browsing, you're not selecting).
- **Story 5-8 (isNew badge)** — added `new-badge` data-testid + isNew prop. We preserve. Fades out on hover per AC #10 collision strategy.
- **Story 10-4 (availability badges)** — added `AvailabilityBadge` component (owned/requested) + isOwned/isRequested props. We preserve. Same fade-out treatment on hover.
- **Story 10-5 (homepage layout responsive)** — set up the `lg:` breakpoint convention this story honors. Hover affordances are `lg:`-only because the responsive grid switches layout there.
- **Sally / Bob / Winston / Amelia / Murat Party Mode (2026-05-08)** — established that drift IS systemic, not isolated. This story is the first under the new Rule 21 enforcement. The bar is high — this becomes the reference implementation.

### Git Intelligence

- **Recent commits (top 5)**: `2687ddb docs(epic-19): mark 19-1/19-2 done` → `6c0cbf2 docs(epic-19): establish design-drift audit + Rules 21/22` → `ab3657f docs(bugfix-10-3): close Bucket A` → `276f519 docs(story): bootstrap bugfix-10-3-skeleton-flicker-on-load to ready-for-dev` → `43cf108 fix(code-review): address bugfix-10-2 findings`. Pattern: small focused commits, distinct types per phase (docs/story-bootstrap/fix-impl/fix-CR), each commit references its scope `(bugfix-10-X)` or `(epic-19)`.
- **Branching**: this story chains off `main` HEAD `2687ddb` (Rule 21 already in `project-context.md` per the prereq). Suggested commit messages:
  1. `docs(story): bootstrap bugfix-10-4-hover-preview-viewport-flip to ready-for-dev` (this story file landing — created by THIS workflow)
  2. `feat(media): bugfix-10-4 PosterCard hover state aligned with .pen MQbvp` (Task 3 — main implementation)
  3. `test(media): bugfix-10-4 migrate hover tests to in-card overlay structure` (Task 4)
  4. `chore(media): bugfix-10-4 delete HoverPreviewCard — superseded by in-card overlay` (Task 2 — make this its OWN commit so the deletion is a separate, reviewable changeset)
  5. `docs(audit): bugfix-10-4 Rule 22 design-drift comparison artifact` (Task 6 — comparison + audit symlink)
- The deletion commit (Task 2) being separate is important — git blame on a deleted file is more useful when the deletion is its own commit.

### Change Log

| Date       | Change |
|------------|--------|
| 2026-04-20 | bugfix-10-4 filed in sprint-status.yaml as `backlog` with original framing: "PosterCard hover preview floats below card, gets clipped/forces scroll. UX decides flip-up via Radix Popover vs click-to-expand vs inline expansion." |
| 2026-05-08 | ⚠️ SCOPE PIVOT via Party Mode (Sally + Bob + Winston + Amelia + Murat). Sally discovered `.pen` already had `Component/PosterCardHover (MQbvp)` design that production never aligned with. Real bug = perceptual-proximity failure (hover floats outside focal area). Sprint-status comment updated; project-context.md gained Rule 21 + Rule 22 + epic-19 (commit 6c0cbf2 + 2687ddb pushed to origin/main). |
| 2026-05-08 | [@contract-v1] AC #1 stamp: in-card hover overlay layout (center play + top-right kebab + bottom-left title overlay + bottom-right rating + top-left selection-checkbox-when-selectable). Downstream consumers: any future story that touches PosterCard hover layout MUST bump v2 + Change Log entry. The new visual contract supersedes the prior implicit-v0 floating-HoverPreviewCard layout (which is being deleted, not contracted). SM Bob `/create-story` (YOLO) generated comprehensive draft including Task 1 design verification + Task 6 manual UX gate (Sally signs off — DEV cannot self-sign per Rule 22). Story is the inaugural Rule 21 enforcement instance + Rule 22 audit instance. |
